reason: TLR9 type-I IFN is plasmacytoid-DC restricted; BMDM produce minimal IFN-beta via CpG. No cascade in either direction.
Both signal via MyD88->NF-kB (LPS also TRIF); overlapping programs, no clean cascade direction.
Both MyD88-biased; overlapping NF-kB, no clear directional cascade.
Both MyD88-only (TLR9, TLR2); same adaptor, parallel — no cascade.
CpG=TLR9/MyD88, PIC=TLR3/TRIF — orthogonal adaptors, no cascade link.
TLR9->MyD88->NF-kB->autocrine TNF. CpG upstream. NF-kB and TNFR programs overlap -> precondition risk; expected weaker than IFN cascades.
LPS engages the TLR4->TRIF arm -> IRF3 -> IFN-beta. LPS-tubes carry the IFN-beta response (autocrine); IFNb-tubes do not carry the IRF3-direct genes. Upstream = LPS.
Low-dose LPS still triggers TLR4->TRIF->IRF3->IFN-beta; the direction (LPSlo upstream) is unambiguous though IFN-beta induction is weaker than full LPS.
TLR2 has no TRIF arm -> no IRF3 -> no IFN-beta induction. P3CSK does not drive IFN-beta; IFN-beta does not drive P3CSK biology.
polyIC engages TLR3->TRIF->IRF3 directly; IFN-beta is the autocrine product acting via IFNAR. S_PIC (IRF3-direct + ISGs) strictly contains S_IFNb (ISGs) -> cleanest cascade; precondition holds.
No TNF->IFN-beta cross-induction in macrophages; IFNAR and TNFR programs are distinct with no cascade link in BMDM.
Same TLR4 receptor at different doses — a dose-response pair, not a cascade. Neither induces the other.
LPS dual-arm (TRIF+MyD88), P3CSK MyD88-only; LPS engages a broader program but neither induces the other -> not a directional cascade.
Different receptors (TLR4 vs TLR3); both reach TRIF but no induction between them in BMDM.
TLR4->MyD88->NF-kB->autocrine TNF loop. LPS upstream. NF-kB/TNFR overlap -> precondition risk (this cascade failed §24 with curated sets).
Both MyD88-biased; coupled by similarity (§22 SHOULD) but no directional cascade between them.
TLR4 vs TLR3 — separate adaptor axes, no cascade direction.
Low-dose TLR4->NF-kB->TNF. LPSlo upstream. NF-kB/TNFR overlap -> risk.
TLR2/MyD88 vs TLR3/TRIF — orthogonal pathways, no cascade.
TLR2->MyD88->NF-kB->TNF. P3CSK upstream. NF-kB/TNFR overlap -> risk.
polyIC can induce TNF via TRIF->NF-kB, but polyIC's dominant output is type-I IFN not TNF; direction ambiguous -> excluded to stay crystal-clear.
